content: Man, I keep getting screwed by taunt because the wording's wrong. It specifically says forces the target to be PRIORITIZED by attacks, but then it winds up sucking up attacks that they were never a valid target for in the first place. Easiest example is strikes, strike says it can only target front-row enemies, so why is a character in the 3rd row with taunt eating strikes? That wasn't a valid target for my strike, so why is it forcing my strike to hit it? lmao
Like, now I'm going to try and remember it's like that, but not being able to work out what things do based on their actual description is kind of aggravating XD
i don't think that's because the description for taunt is wrong
more like the description for basic attacks are wrong
probably should be something like, "basic attacks always hit the front-row target, unless altered by other effects."
that or i guess taunt's description should be changed to "only affects basic attacks"

There's a lot of incorrectly worded things. Like, fortress says that is reduces ACTIVE DAMAGE by 50%, and so I used Uvash's exalt on them because it doesn't deal damage, it only "inflicts 40 points of bleed". The 40 was reduced to 20 anyway. Clementine's Projections skill says it deals additional INSTANCES of damage for each card in the opponent's hand. The opponent had 2 stacks of barrier and 2 cards in hand, so naturally you'd assume it would hit 3 times, remove 2 barrier, and deal 3 damage. Instead it only removed 1 barrier
etc. etc.

I feel like I'm losing every 1 in 4 draft matches to poor wording. Which isn't the worst but it still feels like I shouldn't really be caring about the mode if there are this many interactions that are typed up objectively wrong XD
I haven't tested taunt with other stuff like Doresain exalt, so I'm not sure which one the incorrect wording is on there
im fairly sure taunt works on basic attacks only
it doesn't affect stuff like, "targets the highest/lowest hp+shield unit"
well, i guess also stuff like targetable affliciton
like if you're choosing to apply bleed to someone
then you can only target the taunter
which is why i only play in chinese 😄
god knows there are a shit ton of text in chiense already i can't even imagine how long the english one's gonna be
Yeah, anything targeted it's working as intended since that's what prioritizing means. If you can target it, you have to target it
That 's why the other interaction's wrong
i haven't tested out clementine's skill
u sure it hits multiple times?
It says "Deal 3 damage to all enemies. For each card in the target's hand, deal an additional 1 INSTANCE of damage to them", so it shouldn't all be getting absorbed by 1 barrier

I guess the poor wording could be the "in the target's hand" part, maybe it only deals additional instances of damage if the cards in hand are from each specific unit, though in my case there was only 1 enemy alive and they had 2 cards in hand that definitely weren't Illusion, so I'm not sure what cards they could have been holding that were both not from the living unit, lol
Actually, I can watch the replay from their view and check

Okay, so I'm not actually sure where the bug is on that one anymore because it looks like it was actually a visual bug that somehow stayed bugged in the replay on both sides. It's saying the "cards" they were holding were an illusion they already used that then magically appeared back in their hand but was unplayable, and a strike from a unit that had been dead for like 3 turns
Which doesn't make it any better honestly, losing because the game told me they were holding x cards when they were actually holding 0 cards is kind of just as bad XD
First of all, target cards with effects such as hit highest HP / lowest HP bypass that gimmick
Also, wheels can give effects to damaging cards and actually give them new effects so it can make it bypass too
Taunt hit only count for selected target without anything else or if it's front row attacks
Doresain for example, his exalt + 3 cost damaging card is front row hit
Same with vortice 1st + 2nd exalt
That's definitely good to know for future matches, doesn't mean it's not worded incorrectly though XD
oh i remember now, i thikn it's "for each card OF SAID CHARACTER in the target's hand, deal an additional 1 instance of damage"
this is the problem with ai translation
without someone going through them and confirming stuff
so like
Technically Uvhash's exalt and other cards are damages or DoT which is a damage over time
if whichever character you're targeting
if the player has 3 cards of said character
you'll do 3 instances of dmg (or additional instances? idk i haven't played her only checked her skills out)
Clem HIT PER UNIT CARDS IN HAND
That's also good to know, again doesn't mean it's not worded incorrectly though. I'm not complaining about how the mechanics work, I'm complaining that they're explained wrong, lmao
that's why it's not as op as it sounded
if your alva have 5 cards in hand and doll have 0,your alva will receive 5 more times the damages, meanwhile doll 0 more times
and yes it's worded incorrectly
5 times more dmg or 5 times more instances of dmg?
XD
i can check but i'm working right now
XD
When i said 5 times more i means the number of hits
That's why it's op against barrier
You force draw then use it to multi hit
And most of their barrier are removed
Then you force discard

For reference, Sorel has the exact same effect as Uvash exalt, but her's is worded in a way that explains its interactions properly. Uvash says "Inflicts 40 points of bleed", Sorel says "Deal 36 damage (converted into) bleed", which would indicate that those two things would work differently, but they don't
